IRE and Merrill College Data Journalism Training

Dow Jones News Fund @ Merrill College

Sunday, June 1 - Sunday, June 8, 2025

(ver May 28, 2025)

Rob Wells, director
David Herzog, co-director
Karen Denny, co-director  
Bridget Lang, assistant

Itinerary

Pre-Conference

  • By Monday May 26: Students were sent data assignments and links to software to load.
  • By May 29: Students profiles due, 11:59 p.m.
  • Join GroupMe for DJNF Data Journalism.

Sunday, June 1, 2025

  • 2 p.m.: Welcome at Knight Hall Eaton Theater.
    • Wells, Herzog, Denny, Lang, and Shirley Carswell, DJNF Executive Director
      • Wifi instructions
        • Logistics. The week ahead. Rooms, meals.
      • Student introductions.
      • Investigate your community: Wells
        • Link to DJNF GitHub repositories

  • 3:30 p.m.: Use data to find stories.

    • Spreadsheet track: Herzog. Emma Ferschweiler, Cooper Gant, Nate Harrington, Ava Hu, Ethan Hylton, Levi Jiang, Sophia Nabours. Eaton Theater
    • Coding track: Wells. Sasha Allen; Hannah Bensen; Olivia Borgula; Declan Bradley; Lauren Lifke; Apurva Mahajan; Patrick McCaslin; Diego Perdomo, Yasmeen Saadi. Room 2103
      • Handout for Coding Track talk

  • 4:30 p.m.: Break and then independent work - Students review data memos for their assigned communities. Produce draft story ideas by 11:59 p.m.

  • 5:30 p.m.: Break

  • 6:00 p.m.: Dinner at Franklins in Hyattsville - (Meet at Knight Hall, board van)

  • 11:59 p.m.: Produce one paragraph draft story idea by 11:59 p.m., emailed to Wells & Herzog

Handouts, Readings for Sunday

  • Welcome Slides

  • Link to DJNF GitHub repositories

  • Investigate your community memo.

  • Link to Jefferson, T. Letter to Edward Carrington, Paris, January 16, 1787

  • Link to The Commission on the Freedom of the Press. Chapter 2: The requirements, pp. 20-29.

  • GitHub Slides

  • Handout for Coding Track talk


Monday, June 2, 2025

  • 8:30 a.m. Breakfast: At Knight Hall

  • 9 a.m.: Meet at Knight Hall

  • Skills sessions. Pick one

    • Coding Track: Data Driven Reporting. (Sean Mussenden, the Howard Center)
      • Bring material into R and make sense of it.
      • Basic webscraping, PDF data extraction
      • Knight Hall Room 2103
    • Spreadsheet Track: Pivot Tables. (Herzog, IRE)
      • A powerful tool to summarize data
      • Knight Hall Room 2107

  • 10:30 a.m.: Break. Coffee / drinks served. Lobby.

  • 11 a.m.: Workshops: Use data to find stories. (Wells, Mussenden, Herzog). Rooms 2103, 2107

  • 12:30 p.m.: Lunch in Knight Hall.

  • 1:30 p.m.: Writing a story pitch memo. All attend. (Denny). Eaton Theater

    • Slides for talk

  • 2:15 p.m.: Hosting a webpage on GitHub. All attend. (Wells/Lang). Eaton Theater

    • Web page hosting tutorial

    • GitHub refresher.

  • 2:45 p.m.: Workshop for story pitch memo due at 11:59 p.m. (Wells, Denny, Herzog, TA coach). Rooms 2103, 2107

  • 4 p.m.: Diversity is Good Journalism: Covering All of the Community. (Prof. Christoph Mergerson). All attend. Room 2103

    • Link to Jefferson, T. Letter to Edward Carrington, Paris, January 16, 1787

    • Link to The Commission on the Freedom of the Press. Chapter 2: The requirements, pp. 20-29

  • 5:30 p.m.: Break

  • 6:00 p.m.: Meet at Knight Hall and walk to Cava, College Park.

  • 11:59 p.m.: Draft story pitch memo emailed to Wells and Herzog.

    Monday, June 3, 11:59 p.m. Draft a story pitch memo.

–Submit as a link to an R Markdown document on your GitHub account.

A minimum of 400 words, building on the story idea paragraph, that incorporates some of the best practices in pitching a story and generally follows the story pitch template and process as described in Monday’s lecture. This draft pitch will reflect a deeper exploration of the assigned data and hopefully some independent research. The best story pitches will have some context about recent coverage of this topic.

Handouts, Readings for Monday -

  • Readings for Cheryl W. Thompson talk

  • Slides for Karen Denny talk

  • Web page hosting tutorial

  • Sean Mussenden R Intro

  • Christoph Mergerson slides

  • Christoph Mergerson contact: cm495@umd.edu

  • Cheryl W. Thompson contact: cwthompson@npr.org


Tuesday June 3, 2025

  • 8:00 a.m. Breakfast: At Knight Hall

  • Heather Taylor, DJ News Fund visiting

  • 9 a.m.-10:15 a.m.: Data Visualization. Using Datawrapper for data visualization. (Adam Marton). All attend. Eaton Theater

  • 10:30 a.m.: Break. Coffee served.

    • Heather Taylor, manager of digital media and programs, Dow Jones News Fund.

  • 11 a.m.: Workshop. Students work on data gathering story pitches. (Wells, Marton, TA as coaches). Eaton Theater

  • 11:45 p.m. - 12:45 p.m.: Guest speaker: Cheryl W. Thompson, investigative correspondent for NPR. All attend. Eaton Theater.

    • Readings before Cheryl Thompson talk

  • 12:45 p.m. - 1:30 p.m. Lunch with Cheryl Thompson. Sandwiches served in Knight Hall.

  • 1:30 p.m. - 3:00 p.m.: Skills sessions

    • Coding Track: Using AI with R. (Derek Willis, the Howard Center)
      • Using AI to extract information from PDFs/images/video/audio
      • Knight Hall Room 2103
    • Spreadsheet Track: Data cleaning. (Herzog, IRE)
      • Use Google Sheets, Open Refine to clean data
      • Knight Hall Room 2107

  • 3:00 p.m.-3:15 p.m. : Break

  • 3:15 p.m.-4:15 p.m.

    • Mapping. Herzog.
      • Make a map in Datawrapper.
      • Knight Hall Room 2103
    • Workshop on AI (Willis / Wells /Lang). Room 2107

  • 4:15 p.m.-5:30 p.m. Workshop

    • Git Ignore!

  • 5:30 p.m.: Break

  • 6:30 p.m.: Dinner at Greene Turtle, College Park, 7356 Baltimore Ave, College Park, MD 20740. (Meet at Knight Hall, walk to venue)

Handouts, Readings for Tuesday -

  • Slides from Cheryl W. Thompson talk
  • Cheryl W Thompson email: cwthompson@npr.org
  • Derek Willis AI and coding lecture
  • Adam Marton’s visualization slides and Datawrapper API repo


Wednesday June 4, 2025

  • 8:30 a.m. Breakfast: At Knight Hall

  • 9 a.m.-10:15 a.m.: Skills sessions

    • Coding Track: Using AI with R. (Daniel Trielli, Merrill College)
      • Use R to link to a LLM and clean data.
      • Knight Hall Room 2103
    • Spreadsheet Track: File Types and Metadata. (Herzog, IRE)
      • Use Microsoft Visual Studio to identify file types
      • Knight Hall Room 2107

  • 10:15 a.m.: Break. Coffee served.

  • 10:30 a.m.: Workshop. Open workshop on projects. (Wells, Herzog, Lang coach). Rooms 2103, 2107

  • 12 p.m.: Lunch in Knight Hall.

    • Brief remarks by Alex Pyles, Associate Dean, Academic Affairs, Philip Merrill College of Journalism

  • 1 p.m. - 2:15 p.m.: Covering Congress remotely, Ed Kachinske, U.S. House Press Gallery, via Zoom. Eaton Theater

    • Exercise: Examine spending by a member of Congress

  • 2:15 p.m. - 2:30 p.m.: Break

  • 2:30 p.m. - 3:30 p.m. Connie Mitchell Ford on business reporting. All attend. Eaton Theater.

    • Three ways to get business and economic data and how to localize it.

  • 3:30 p.m.: Workshop. Connie Mitchell Ford workshop on project proposals. (Wells, Lang, Herzog as coaches). Eaton Theater

  • 5 p.m.: Break

  • 6:00 p.m.: Dinner delivered to Knight Hall. Taqueria Habanero

  • 11:59 p.m.: Second draft of story pitch memos due, emailed to Wells and Herzog.

Handouts, Readings for Wednesday -

  • Daniel Trielli slides

  • Constance Ford slides


Thursday June 5, 2025

  • 7:45 a.m. Breakfast: At Knight Hall
  • 8:15 a.m.: Meet at Knight Hall, board van, depart for College Park Metro (Wells/Herzog driving)
  • 10 a.m.: Tour of AP D.C. bureau with Chad Day, AP’s elections team.
  • Noon: Lunch.
  • 1:30 p.m.: Tour U.S. Capitol press galleries with Stephen Neukam, Axios and Ed Kachinske, U.S. House Press Gallery.
  • 6 p.m.: Dinner in Washington D.C.

Handouts, Readings for Thursday -

  • Chad Day, AP: cday@ap.org

  • Gary Fields, AP: GFields@AP.org

  • Stephen Neukam, Axios: stephen.neukam@axios.com

  • Ed Kachinske, House Press Gallery: Edward.Kachinske@mail.house.gov


Friday June 6, 2025

  • 8:30 a.m. Breakfast: At Knight Hall
  • 9 a.m.: Gathering Public Records
    • All attend. (Herzog and Wells). Eaton Theater
  • 10:00 a.m. - 10:15 a.m.: Break
  • 10:15 a.m.: Workshop on projects.
  • 11:15 a.m.: Skills to survive in the newsroom. Time management, deadlines. All attend. (Denny) Eaton Theater
  • 12 p.m.: Lunch in Knight Hall.
    • Speaker: Prof. Kevin Blackistone on racial justice in sports business
  • 1 p.m.: Workshop on projects (Wells, Herzog, Denny, Lang coach)
  • 2:45 p.m.: Meet at Knight Hall, board van, depart for College Park Metro, dinner in Washington D.C., attend a community jazz show at Westminster Church.
  • 4:30 p.m. Meet with Rev. Brian Hamilton at Westminster Church to discuss Southwest D.C. history.
  • 6 p.m. Jazz show and dinner at Westminister Church: The Organization. Greg Hatza, organ; Brian Kooken, guitar; Robert Shahid, drums; Dred Scott, vocals

Handouts, Readings for Friday -

  • David Herzog, Open Records

  • Karen Denny, [How to Succeed in a Newsroom] https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1D-E8yd_BARkJ100iMOjedThaHBvbD15qPrv0IclgmV8/edit?usp=sharing

  • Kevin Blackistone, Sports and Environmental Justice

  • Kevin Blackistone columns


Saturday June 7, 2025

  • 9:00 a.m. Breakfast: At Knight Hall

  • 10:00 a.m.: Students present web pages, story pitches, solicit feedback.

  • 12:15 p.m.: Lunch in Knight Hall

  • 1 p.m.: On the van for D.C.

  • 2:15 p.m.: Tour of African-American History Museum, D.C.

  • 6:00 p.m.: Dinner at Ledo Pizza, College Park

Handouts, Readings for Saturday - TK


Sunday June 8, 2025

  • Breakfast on your own.
  • Depart on your own schedule

Residence Halls & Campus

  • You are staying in Dorchester Hall, a very short walk to Knight Hall. The 24-hospitality (check-in/out) desk is located in Queen Anne’s Hall, a short walk to the south. The desk phone number is 301-314-4455 (HILL) for key- and access-related questions. The Stamp Student Union, with a food court and various retail, is near Dorchester Hall.
